name: Carrilet I (Olot - Girona)
description:
Carrilet I greenway is a route that follows the old route of the train that connected Olot and Girona. It is an ideal route to do by bike or on foot, as it has little gradient and crosses very varied and beautiful landscapes. The route has a length of 56 kilometers and passes through twelve towns in three counties: Garrotxa, Gironès and Selva.

The route starts in Olot, in the heart of the Garrotxa volcanic area, where you can admire the natural and cultural heritage of this region. From Olot, the route goes to Les Preses, Sant Esteve d'en Bas and Sant Feliu de Pallerols, where you can see the Hostoles castle. Afterwards, the route climbs up to the En Bas pass, the highest point on the itinerary, at 589 meters above sea level. From there, the route gently descends to Girona, following the valleys of the rivers Fluvià, Brugent and Ter.

Along the way, you can visit charming towns such as Les Planes d'Hostoles, Amer, La Cellera de Ter, Anglès and Bonmatí. Finally, the route reaches Girona, where you can enjoy the history and culture of these cities. In Girona, the route connects with the second section of Carrilet, which goes to Sant Feliu de Guíxols.

More information: www.viesverdes.cat
region_intro: Hiking around Olot offers exploration of a unique volcanic landscape within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. The region features numerous dormant volcanic cones, ancient lava flows, and diverse forests. These geological formations create varied terrain, including volcanic hummocks and distinct craters. The landscape is characterized by a mix of holm-oak, common oak, and beech forests growing on volcanic soil. Best hiking trails around Olot The most popular hiking route is Volcán de Santa Margarida and Volcán del Croscat - Circular through the Natural Park of the Volcanic Zone of La Garrotxa, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 24 minutes to complete. This route explores two prominent volcanoes, including the unique Santa Margarida with its hermitage inside the crater. Another top favourite among local hikers is Volcà Croscat – Croscat Volcano Scoria Slopes loop from Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.3 km) path. This trail provides views of Croscat, the tallest and youngest volcano in the park, known for its "sliced" appearance revealing its interior. Local hikers also love the Santa Margarida Volcano Crater – Santa Margarida Volcano loop from Garrotxa, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail leading through the circular crater of Santa Margarida Volcano, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Olot is defined by dormant volcanic cones, ancient lava flows, and lush beech forests like Fageda d'en Jordà. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks across volcanic terrain. The routes in Olot are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 3,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Olot's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Olot?
A: Olot, situated within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, offers a diverse range of hiking trails. You'll find paths that traverse dormant volcanic cones, ancient lava flows, and lush forests, including holm-oak, common oak, and the unique beech forest of Fageda d'en Jordà. The terrain varies, providing options from gentle strolls to more challenging treks across volcanic landscapes.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for families with children in Olot?
A: Yes, Olot has many family-friendly options. Out of over 110 hiking routes, nearly 50 are classified as easy. A great choice for families is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40332378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fageda d'en Jordà trailhead – Croscat Volcano Scoria Slopes loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 6.2 km (3.9 miles) path through the enchanting beech forest and near the Croscat Volcano.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park?
A: Many trails in the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park are dog-friendly, allowing you to explore the unique volcanic landscape with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Specific regulations may apply in certain protected areas, so check local signage.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking around Olot?
A: The region is rich in unique natural features. You can explore several dormant volcanoes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida Volcano&lt;/a&gt;, famous for its hermitage inside the crater, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4052842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Croscat Volcano Scoria Slopes&lt;/a&gt;, which reveals its interior due to past mining. Don't miss the enchanting Fageda d'en Jordà beech forest, growing on a lava flow with its distinctive volcanic hummocks. For stunning views, consider the Ruta dels Miradors (Viewpoints Route) in Olot, with the Montsacopa volcano summit offering 360-degree panoramas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Olot?
A: Yes, many popular routes around Olot are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A highly recommended circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918698190/volcan-de-santa-margarida-and-volcan-del-croscat-circular-through-the-natural-park-of-the-volcanic-zone-of-la-garrotxa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Volcán de Santa Margarida and Volcán del Croscat circular trail&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 12.1 km (7.5 miles) and takes you through two prominent volcanoes and the Fageda d'en Jordà.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Olot?
A: Olot offers great hiking opportunities throughout the year, but spring and autumn are particularly pleasant. In spring, the landscape is vibrant and green, while autumn transforms the Fageda d'en Jordà beech forest into a spectacular display of colors. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are advisable. Winter hiking is also possible, with cooler temperatures and a different kind of serene beauty.

Q: How long do hikes around Olot typically take?
A: Hiking durations vary significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. Easy trails, like those through Fageda d'en Jordà, might take 1.5 to 2 hours. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31946151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida Volcano Crater loop&lt;/a&gt; (11.5 km / 7.1 miles), typically take around 3 to 3.5 hours. More challenging or longer combined routes can extend to half a day or more.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural pools accessible via hiking trails near Olot?
A: Yes, the area around Olot features several beautiful waterfalls and natural pools. You can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4391819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molí dels Murris Pool&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3304941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Cotilla Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. These natural attractions often make for refreshing stops during your hike and are popular spots to enjoy the natural beauty of the region.

Q: What are the parking options for hikers visiting the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park?
A: There are designated parking areas available at key access points to the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, especially near popular trailheads like those leading to Santa Margarida and Croscat volcanoes, and the Fageda d'en Jordà. Some parking lots may have a small fee, particularly during peak season, to help maintain the park. Always check local signage for specific parking regulations.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails in Olot using public transport?
A: Olot itself is accessible by bus from major cities like Barcelona and Girona. Once in Olot, local bus services or walking can get you to some trailheads. For routes deeper within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, public transport options might be limited, and a car is often more convenient for direct access to specific starting points. However, the former railway line connecting Olot to Girona has been converted into a greenway, which is accessible by public transport at various points and popular for walking.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes in Olot?
A: The hiking trails around Olot cater to all levels of experience. Out of over 110 routes, about 47 are considered easy, 54 are moderate, and 11 are more difficult. This means you can find anything from gentle walks through forests and around volcano bases to more strenuous treks with greater elevation changes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31853454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Valentí Shrine Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 230 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Olot?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique volcanic landscapes, the enchanting Fageda d'en Jordà beech forest, and the well-maintained trails that offer a mix of natural beauty and geological interest. The opportunity to explore dormant volcanoes and their craters is a particular highlight for many.
tips: This section of the Via Verde is very pleasant. Lots of shade, very slight slope when not absent and (relatively) far from cars and noise. The ground is good, sometimes there are paths with rocks on the surface but nothing uncomfortable.
The Col de Bas is a very small pass, the descent to Olot is magnificent. It peaks at 597m above sea level and marks the entrance to the Garrotxa volcanic zone natural park.
Via Verda in the woods towards olot

Amer is located halfway between Olot and Girona and is a town with more than a thousand years of history, where you can still breathe its medieval past. Amer has been the birthplace of several presidents of the Generalitat; four were abbots of the monastery between the 15th and 17th centuries, and two were born in Amer. The Amer Valley has great forest wealth, and the forests of chestnut, hazel, oak and pine stand out. Bordering the eastern part of the town is the Brugent River, formerly called the Amera River, which merges with the Ter River. The Carrilet route is one of the access entrances to the town. Its old railway station is today a mandatory stop and in the past it was one of the most important, since the train workshops and warehouses were also located here.
Metal and wood bridge that crosses a small ravine next to the River Ter, near the town of Bonmatí.
